headline: Disney Stock Drops. Why Layoffs Won’t Be Enough for Investors.
summary: Walt Disney  is planning to cut more jobs—but new CEO Josh D’Amaro will need to take bigger swings than that to turn the entertainment giant’s long-suffering stock around.  Disney is preparing to ax as many as 1,000 positions in the next few weeks, The Wall Street Journal reported late Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.  Many of the positions eliminated will be in Disney’s marketing department and plans for the layoffs began before D’Amaro replaced former CEO Bob Iger, per the report.

headline: Is Disney (DIS) Fairly Priced After Recent 9.7% Annual Share Price Gain?
summary: Wondering whether Walt Disney at around US$99 per share is offering good value right now, or if the price already reflects the story you believe in. The stock has returned 2.7% over the last 7 days, while the 1 year return sits at 9.7% and the 5 year return shows a 45.8% decline, which can change how you think about both potential growth and risk. headline: Disney Planning Layoffs Under New CEO D’Amaro
summary: Disney  is preparing to make sizable layoffs in one of the first significant moves under the new chief executive officer,  Josh D’Amaro.  The entertainment company is planning to eliminate as many as 1,000 positions in the coming weeks, according to people familiar with the matter.  Like many Hollywood studios, Disney has been trying to adjust to the smaller profits it earns from streaming compared with what it used to make in linear television, as well as diminished box office and intense competition from such tech companies as  Amazon.com  and Google’s YouTube.

Like many Hollywood studios, Disney has been trying to adjust to the smaller profits it earns from streaming compared with what it used to make in linear television, as well as diminished box office and intense competition from such tech companies as  Amazon.com  and Google’s YouTube.
